summary: The AIM Problem Lists dataset contains 101 open problems spanning 80+ mathematics topics, with the sampled issues showing a striking pattern: all visible problems have 0 open and 1 resolved issue, each tagged as a 'Proof' of the corresponding problem. The proof status fields (q1-q10) each report 100 issues, suggesting a uniform, large-scale verification or proof-tracking effort across the dataset. Notable traction appears in additive combinatorics (aim_addcombapp series) and hypergraph Turán problems.
problems: Many issues are untitled (e.g. 'Proof of aim_addcombapp_0001: (untitled)'), making it hard to assess problem content or distinguish progress
Inconsistent problem ID formatting mixes zero-padded (aim_addcombapp_0001) and non-padded (aim_addcombapp_001) identifiers, risking duplicate or fragmented tracking
The proof status breakdown (q1-q10 each with 100 issues) is opaque — it is unclear what the q-fields represent or how they map to specific problems
No problems currently show open issues in the sample, which may indicate either genuine resolution or stale/uncatalogued status that hides active work
highlights: Every sampled problem shows a resolved proof issue (1 resolved, 0 open), indicating strong completion momentum
The aim_addcombapp series shows the most concentrated activity with at least six distinct tracked problems
aim_addcombapp_0002 ('3APs in sets of small growth') has a descriptive, content-rich title indicating a well-specified and likely near-complete result
Broad topical coverage (spectral theory, combinatorics, number theory, geometry) provides diverse opportunities for parallel progress

suggested_todos.title: Add descriptive titles to all '(untitled)' proof issues to enable meaningful progress tracking
Standardize problem ID formatting (resolve zero-padding inconsistency) to prevent duplicate records
Document the meaning of the q1-q10 proof status fields and link them to specific problems
Review aim_addcombapp_0002 ('3APs in sets of small growth') for final verification and publication readiness
Audit problems showing 0 open / 1 resolved to confirm genuine resolution versus stale status
Prioritize the additive combinatorics cluster for a focused review session given its concentrated activity
